taskCaption: object starts near the container and is placed inside it (relation-event diagnostic)

pilotMetadata.tests: Asserts, via the FROZEN matcher: the mover's TERMINAL relation to the container is INSIDE (hard OBJECT_RELATION_GOAL -> goal_satisfaction); the mover STARTED NEAR, not already inside (initial_state, from the authored r0 NEAR endpoint); it ENDED INSIDE (terminal_state + relation_transitions); and one CONTAINMENT_CHANGE event is present (event_presence). One structural step beyond terminal-only: it requires the object to have STARTED near and ACHIEVED INSIDE, not merely to be inside at the end. No contact, hand, co-motion, release, or temporal order.
addedOverTerminalOnly: relations(authored NEAR r0 + INSIDE r1) -> contributes initial_state(NEAR) + terminal_state(INSIDE)
pilotMetadata.enforcedVsAuthoring: CRITICAL: a 'born-inside' episode is rejected by initial_state, NOT by relation_transitions/event_presence. csg/rollout_extract.py seeds prev_rel='NEAR' UNCONDITIONALLY, so an object that ends INSIDE -- even one inside the whole time -- ALWAYS emits a NEAR->INSIDE CONTAINMENT_CHANGE delta. The only thing distinguishing a real put-in from born-inside is the robot's FIRST relation fact (NEAR for a real put-in, INSIDE for born-inside), caught by initial_state. ENFORCED: terminal INSIDE, INSIDE achieved (relation_transitions), initial NEAR endpoint (initial_state), CONTAINMENT_CHANGE present (event_presence). event_order stays support 0 (one event, no pair to order against). AUTHORING ONLY (not verifier-enforced): timeSpan magnitudes, confidences, geometry source/category.
pilotMetadata.deferredNote: The human-reviewed RH20T annotation has no honest evidence for who/what caused the motion; asserting contact or causal order would be unevidenced. Those remain a later contact/order target.
pilotMetadata.source: RH20T selected containment episode; raw media stays on RunPod. See datasets/rh20t_object_inside_container_v0/manifest.json.
pilotMetadata.notAGoldTask: Pilot diagnostic, deliberately NOT added to gold_tests/. It asserts the object started near and was placed inside the container; it does NOT assert the agent contacted or caused the motion. Read it for relation-event mappability from an external recorded source, not as a full object_inside_container acceptance test.
